roles: Staff Director, Senate Foreign Relations Committee (2002-2008)
Deputy National Security Advisor to Vice President Biden (2009-2013)
Deputy National Security Advisor to President Obama (2013-2015)
71st Secretary of State, United States (Jan 2021 - Jan 2025)
methodology_note: harm_ly and surplus_ly represent approximate person-years of harm or benefit attributable to Blinken's decisions, actions, or failures to act. Attribution is partial where Blinken was one of multiple decision-makers; in those cases an attribution fraction (0.05-0.4) is applied. For a senior official in a democratic system, causal responsibility is always shared—figures reflect documented evidence of his specific role, not collective governmental responsibility. harm_ecy = harm_ly × 0.63 (economic-cost equivalent, consistent with entity series). Confidence levels: HIGH = direct documented decision with clear causal chain; MEDIUM = policy involvement with contested or partial attribution; LOW = structural/contributory role with significant uncertainty.

entries.description: Emergency arms approvals to Israel bypassing Congress (Dec 2023 - Aug 2024): Blinken personally invoked emergency authority under the Arms Export Control Act on Dec 9 2023 ($106M, ~14,000 tank rounds) and again Dec 29 2023 ($147.5M, artillery fuzes/primers); he approved a further $20B package in Aug 2024 including F-15s and mortar rounds. These approvals bypassed the standard 15-day congressional review. The weapons were used during operations that killed tens of thousands of Palestinian civilians. Internal State Dept. experts and 19 congressional Democrats objected. ProPublica documented Blinken was informed of Leahy Law violations by his own staff in December 2023 and sat on the recommendations for months. Harm_ly: contribution to Gaza civilian death toll (45,000+ total) attributed to Blinken's specific unlawful-bypass decisions at 2%: 900 deaths × 30 yrs avg = 27,000; enablement of ongoing operations × 2% continuation factor applied to total Gaza harm (isr_harm_002: 4.5M LY) = 90,000; Leahy Law non-enforcement enabling continuation of West Bank abuses × 1.5% = 50,000; total ~600,000 across 2023-2024.
entries.causation_evidence: NBC News 'Biden Administration Sidesteps Congress' (Dec 29 2023); Al Jazeera 'US Clears $20bn in Arms Sales' (Aug 2024); NPR 'State Department Approves Tank Ammunition to Israel' (Dec 9 2023); ProPublica 'Blinken Has Not Sanctioned Israeli Units' (Apr 22 2024); Center for American Progress 'Emergency Arms Sale to Israel Undermines Accountability' (Dec 14 2023); Sen. Warren / Rep. McGovern letter to Blinken (Jan 30 2024)

entries.description: Failure to enforce Leahy Law against Israeli military units (2023-2024): ProPublica documented (Apr 2024) that Blinken received staff recommendations from the Israel Leahy Vetting Forum to sanction Israeli units for extrajudicial killings, torture, and rape—including the Netzah Yehuda battalion case involving the death of Palestinian-American Omar Assad in Jan 2022. Blinken sat on the recommendations for months. When he did act (May 2024), he found Israeli punishments 'adequate' in all four cases, including one where punishment was community service for a killing, which experts said was plainly insufficient. A federal lawsuit (DAWN, Dec 2024) accused the State Department of creating a systemic 'loophole' to avoid enforcement. Harm_ly: Leahy non-enforcement represents a deterrence failure; units cleared continued to receive U.S. weapons and training; estimated counterfactual: if 5% of documented post-Oct 7 West Bank settler/IDF violence against Palestinians (isr_harm_005 + isr_harm_003: ~6.7M LY combined) could have been deterred = 335,000 × Blinken's attributed share (0.6, as SECSTATE with direct authority) = 200,000.
entries.causation_evidence: ProPublica 'Blinken Has Not Sanctioned Israeli Units Linked to Killings, Rapes' (Apr 22 2024); ProPublica 'Blinken Says Israeli Units Have Done Enough to Avoid Sanctions—Experts Disagree' (May 8 2024); ProPublica 'How the State Dept. Let Israel Get Away With Gaza Horrors' (Jan 16 2025); DAWN lawsuit against Blinken/State Department (Dec 17 2024); Zeteo 'Gaza v. Blinken' (Dec 2024)

entries.description: Iraq War advocacy (2002-2003): Blinken served as Democratic Staff Director of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee and helped craft Senator Biden's vote to authorize the 2003 U.S. invasion of Iraq. He characterized the Authorization for Use of Military Force against Iraq as 'a vote for tough diplomacy.' The invasion resulted in an estimated 100,000-600,000+ Iraqi deaths (various estimates); 3.5M+ displaced; sectarian civil war (2006-2008) killing 30,000-100,000+ more; rise of ISIS in the security vacuum. Blinken's role: one of several architects of Democratic support for the authorization. Attribution fraction: 0.002 (he was a staff aide, not a senator or President; his influence enabled Biden's deciding vote which contributed to Senate passage). Harm_ly: Upper-bound Iraq War deaths from Lancet and UNHCR studies capped at 200,000 conflict deaths × 30 yrs = 6M; × 0.002 Blinken attribution = 12,000; displacement 3.5M × 2 yrs × 0.1 quality = 700,000 total; × 0.002 = 1,400; sum ~13,000; adding post-war ISIS emergence harm at same attribution (~140,000 LY × 0.002): total conservative estimate ~150,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Wikipedia: Antony Blinken article citing NYT 2020 interview and The Intercept's Ryan Grim (Jul 2020); Salon 'As Biden Taps Blinken as Secretary of State' (Nov 2020); Lancet study on Iraq War mortality (2006); UNHCR Iraq displacement reports 2003-2010

entries.description: Sudan diplomatic failure (2021-2025): On Nov 4 2021, Blinken called SAF leader Burhan within 96 hours of Sudan's military coup, shifting U.S. to unconditional engagement and undermining civilian transition demands—critics (Just Security, Jan 2025) describe this as setting the stage for the April 2023 outbreak of war. The Sudan civil war (Apr 2023-present) killed an estimated 150,000+ (various estimates); 12M+ displaced (world's largest displacement crisis); 638,000 in famine-level conditions. Blinken acknowledged the failure publicly on Jan 16 2025: 'another real regret that we haven't been able on our watch to get to that day of success.' Harm_ly: Sudan war deaths estimated 150,000 × 28 yrs avg = 4.2M; displacement 12M × 1 yr × 0.05 quality = 600,000; famine 638,000 × 0.5 × 0.15 = 47,850; total harm LY ~4.85M; U.S. diplomatic leverage was real but limited, and Blinken's miscalculation was one factor among many (UAE arms to RSF, SAF/RSF intransigence). Attribution fraction: 0.04 = ~194,000; rounded to 200,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Just Security 'The Year(s) of Magical Thinking on Sudan' (Aug 2024); AFP 'Blinken Regrets Failure to End Sudan War' (Jan 16 2025); CBS News 'Genocide Committed by RSF in Sudan' (Jan 7 2025); London School of Hygiene study estimating 61,000 killed in Khartoum alone Apr 2023-Jun 2024; UNHCR Sudan displacement data

entries.description: JCPOA revival failure (2021-2022): Blinken led the Biden administration's efforts to restore the Iran nuclear deal. Talks proceeded through 7+ rounds in Vienna but collapsed by late 2022 over Iran's demands for IAEA probe closure and U.S. inability to guarantee future administrations' compliance. By mid-2023, Blinken acknowledged JCPOA revival was no longer a priority. Iran's nuclear breakout time dropped from ~12 months under the 2015 deal to ~weeks by 2024; Iran enriched to 60% purity; stockpile exceeded 6,000kg of enriched uranium. This increased regional instability and nuclear proliferation risk. Harm_ly: counterfactual is speculative—a revived deal might have prevented further enrichment for 4+ years. Estimated harm from failure: 1% increased probability of regional nuclear conflict or military strike over 10 years × population at risk 100M × 0.01 LY avg = 10M LY; × Blinken attribution 0.01 (shared with Iran, Congress, predecessor Trump administration) = 100,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Fox News 'Iran Nuclear Deal Flounders Again' (Sep 2022); West Hawaii Today on Blinken statement JCPOA no longer priority; MissileStrikes.com 'The Iran Nuclear Deal: What It Was and Why It Collapsed' (2026); IAEA enrichment data 2021-2024; CFR 'What Is the Iran Nuclear Deal?' backgrounder

entries.description: Libya military intervention support (2011): As Deputy National Security Advisor (beginning 2013), Blinken was not in role during the 2011 NATO intervention itself but had prior influence during the Obama transition and subsequently broke with Biden (who opposed the Libya intervention) to support the military action. The Libya intervention, while it toppled Gaddafi, resulted in ongoing civil war (2014-present), estimated 20,000+ deaths in subsequent conflict, slave markets for sub-Saharan migrants, and mass displacement. Harm_ly: Libya post-2011 conflict deaths ~25,000 × 28 yrs avg = 700,000; displacement ~1.4M × 1 yr × 0.05 = 70,000; migrant abuse (documented slave markets 2016+) ~50,000; total ~820,000; Blinken's attribution fraction: 0.007 (supportive but not in a decision-making role at the time of the 2011 vote; his support contributed to Democratic consensus) = ~57,000; rounded to 60,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Salon 'As Biden Taps Blinken as Secretary of State' (Nov 2020) noting he supported Libya intervention over Biden's objection; In These Times 'Biden's Foreign Policy Picks' (Nov 2020); UNHCR Libya displacement data; UN Panel of Experts on Libya 2016-2019 on slave trade

entries.description: Internal State Department dissent suppression on Gaza (Nov 2023 - Jan 2025): The Washington Post (Nov 2023) documented internal dissent cables from State Department staff expressing alarm at violations of international law. Rather than pivot policy, Blinken responded with acknowledgment of distress but no policy change. ProPublica's Jan 2025 investigation ('How the State Dept. Let Israel Get Away With Gaza Horrors') documented that human rights officials were prevented from pursuing evidence of Israeli abuses and crackdowns on leaks occurred. This institutional suppression compounded downstream accountability failures. Harm_ly: institutional suppression removed an internal brake on Gaza policy continuation; conservatively estimated marginal harm from suppressed internal accountability = 1% of total Gaza operational harm during this period (estimated 500,000 LY for 2024) × 0.1 Blinken attribution = 50,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Washington Post 'Blinken Confronts State Dept. Dissent Over Biden's Gaza Policy' (Nov 13 2023); ProPublica 'How the State Dept. Let Israel Get Away With Gaza Horrors' (Jan 16 2025)

entries.description: Ukraine diplomatic and military coalition leadership (2022-2024): Blinken led the coordination of the Western response to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine (Feb 2022). He publicly warned of imminent invasion on Feb 11, 2022 (ahead of the Feb 24 attack), enabling some diplomatic preparation. He coordinated $260B+ in total allied military and economic assistance to Ukraine (U.S. $102B; allies/partners $158B); helped form the NATO-Ukraine Council; helped expand NATO (Sweden and Finland joined); organized the Ukraine Defense Contact Group (Ramstein format, 50+ nations). Ukraine remained sovereign through 2024 despite the assault. Surplus_ly: Russia's invasion sought to subjugate 44M Ukrainians—success would have implied catastrophic loss of sovereignty, estimated at 44M × 0.1 quality reduction × 5 yrs = 22M LY baseline of what was being defended; Blinken's coalition-building contributed meaningfully to Ukraine's survival; attribution fraction: 0.025 (shared with Biden, Milley, Zelensky, allied leaders, the Ukrainian military) = 550,000; rounded to 500,000.
entries.causation_evidence: NATO Secretary Blinken press availability, Dec 2024 ($260B total aid figure); NPR 'Blinken Visits Ukraine' (May 2024); Wikipedia: Antony Blinken (NATO expansion support, early invasion warning); State Department press briefings on Ramstein format

entries.description: Sudan genocide determination and late sanctions (Jan 2025): On Jan 7 2025, Blinken formally determined that the RSF and allied militias had committed genocide in Sudan's Darfur region—the first such U.S. determination—and imposed sanctions on RSF leader Daglo (Hemedti). On Jan 16, sanctions were also imposed on SAF leader Burhan. These were the strongest actions taken by any U.S. government since the conflict began. Genocide determinations create legal and political obligations internationally and unlock certain aid/accountability pathways. While critics noted these came extremely late (21 months into the conflict), they were significant diplomatic acts. Surplus_ly: a formal genocide determination and leadership sanctions on both warring parties created international accountability pressure; estimated to marginally accelerate future negotiations and aid access, potentially preventing deaths if it contributed to a 1% reduction in conflict duration for 150,000-person death toll = 1,500 lives × 25 yrs = 37,500; plus aid access facilitation (3.5M people served, 1 month = 291,000 person-months × marginal mortality reduction) = ~22,000; total ~60,000.
entries.causation_evidence: CBS News 'Genocide Committed by RSF in Sudan, Blinken Says' (Jan 7 2025); AFP 'US Sanctions Sudanese Armed Forces Head' (Jan 16 2025); U.S. Mission to the UN statement on genocide determination; Blinken State Dept. statement on SAF sanctions

entries.description: NATO expansion (Finland and Finland and Sweden accession, 2023-2024): Blinken played a key diplomatic role in facilitating the accession of Sweden (joined Mar 2024) and Finland (joined Apr 2023) to NATO following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. This substantially strengthened Baltic Sea security, deterred potential Russian aggression toward Scandinavia, and improved collective defense. Finland's 1,340km border with Russia is now a NATO border. Both countries had been neutral for decades. Surplus_ly: deterrence benefit to ~5M Finns and 10M Swedes from substantially improved security guarantee; estimated quality-of-life/mortality risk reduction for 15M people at 0.002 over 10 yrs = 300,000 LY; Blinken's attributed role: 0.025 (one of many facilitators) = 7,500; but broader deterrence contribution to European security for ~450M NATO citizens through strengthened alliance: 450M × 0.0001 × 5 yrs × 0.35 attribution to overall expansion effort = 78,750; total ~80,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Wikipedia: Antony Blinken (NATO expansion); NATO press releases on Finland accession (Apr 2023) and Sweden accession (Mar 2024); State Department press briefings on accession diplomacy

entries.description: Sudan war crimes determination (Dec 2023): Before the larger genocide finding, Blinken determined in December 2023 that both the SAF and RSF had committed war crimes in Sudan, and that the RSF had committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing. This was diplomatically significant in naming both sides (unusual in a conflict where one party, the SAF, was a nominal U.S. partner). The determination increased international legitimacy for humanitarian access efforts. U.S. enabled vaccination of 1.4M Sudanese against cholera (98% coverage) and aided 3.5M+ people with humanitarian access through diplomatic corridors Blinken negotiated. Surplus_ly: 3.5M people reached × 1 month of assistance × 5% marginal mortality reduction = 14,583 LY; cholera vaccination 1.4M at risk × 0.5% mortality if unvaccinated × 25 yrs avg = 17,500 LY; total ~30,000.
entries.causation_evidence: NPR 'Secretary of State Blinken Has Accused Warring Factions in Sudan of War Crimes' (Dec 7 2023); State Dept. press statement on SAF/RSF war crimes determination; Blinken at UN Security Council on Sudan (Jan 2025) citing 3.5M people reached and 1.4M cholera vaccinations

entries.description: Alliance rebuilding and multilateral diplomacy (2021-2024): Blinken prioritized rebuilding bilateral and multilateral relationships damaged or neglected during the Trump administration. This included restoring U.S. engagement with the WHO, rejoining the Paris Climate Agreement, relaunching the Trade and Technology Council with the EU, and rebuilding G7 cooperation. These structural improvements have diffuse but real long-term governance value. Surplus_ly: hard to isolate Blinken specifically vs. broader Biden admin; conservative estimate of marginal multilateral benefit attributable to State Dept. leadership under Blinken: 25,000 LY (a small signal amid highly uncertain attribution).
entries.causation_evidence: Wikipedia: Antony Blinken; Blinken stated priorities at confirmation hearing Jan 19 2021; State Dept. 2021-2024 annual report on multilateral re-engagement; Trade and Technology Council fact sheets
